place: Belvidere town, New Jersey, New Jersey

facts.text: Belvidere’s name comes from Italian, meaning “beautiful to see,” a nod to its picturesque setting along the Delaware River.
As the county seat, Belvidere’s historic courthouse faces a central square that has hosted public hangings and trial proceedings for over two centuries.
Belvidere Cemetery, founded in 1834, houses graves of Civil War figures and is a highlight of the town’s annual Victorian Days tour.
Hotel Belvidere, a family‑owned landmark, has welcomed travelers since the 19th century and remains a centerpiece of the town’s historic charm.
The town’s historic district, celebrated for its long‑standing heritage, showcases architecture that dates back hundreds of years.
The Warren County Museum, now home to the Historical and Genealogical Society, preserves artifacts that chronicle Belvidere’s rich past.
